description:
All audit directories must be owned by root user. By default, the path for audit log is 
/var/log/audit/
. To properly set the owner of /var/log/audit, run the command: 
$ sudo chown root /var/log/audit
rationale:
Unauthorized disclosure of audit records can reveal system and configuration data to
attackers, thus compromising its confidentiality.
ocil_clause: the directory is not owned by root
ocil:
Determine where the audit logs are stored with the following command:

$ sudo grep -iw log_file /etc/audit/auditd.conf

log_file = /var/log/audit/audit.log

Determine the owner of the audit log directory by using the output of the above command
(default: "/var/log/audit/"). Run the following command with the correct audit log directory
path:

$ sudo ls -ld /var/log/audit

drwx------ 2 root root 23 Jun 11 11:56 /var/log/audit

The audit log directory must be owned by "root"
fixtext:
Change the owner of the directory /var/log/audit to root.
Run the following command:

$ sudo chown root [audit_log_directory]

Replace "[audit_log_directory]" with the correct audit log directory path. By default, this
location is usually "/var/log/audit".
srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 audit log directory must be owned by root to prevent unauthorized read access.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 audit log directory must be owned by root to prevent unauthorized read access.
stig.vuldiscussion: Unauthorized disclosure of audit records can reveal system and configuration data to attackers, thus compromising its confidentiality.
stig.checktext:
Verify the audit logs directory is owned by "root".

Determine where the audit logs are stored with the following command:

$ sudo grep -iw log_file /etc/audit/auditd.conf

log_file = /var/log/audit/audit.log

Using the location of the audit log file, determine if the audit log directory is owned by "root" using the following command:

$ sudo stat -c '%U %n' /var/log/audit

root /var/log/audit

If the audit log directory is not owned by "root", this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Configure the audit log to be protected from unauthorized read access by setting the correct owner as "root" with the following command:

$ sudo chown root /var/log/audit

title: System Audit Directories Must Be Owned By Root
